senses.definition: to laugh or scream in great length or with extreme intensity

examples.x: They will be laughing their heads off when they see my costume.
When the stand-up comedian delivered their punchline, the entire audience laughed their heads off.
The funny sitcom had the viewers laughing their heads off throughout the entire episode.
The children watched the clown's performance and laughed their heads off at the silly antics.
During the family game night, the hilarious charades had everyone laughing their heads off, making it a memorable evening.

senses.definition: to laugh so hard to the point that one's eyes become full of tears

examples.x: The stand-up comedian had the entire audience laughing until they cried with his witty jokes and humorous anecdotes.
At the family reunion, the funny stories and memories had everyone laughing till they cried.
Watching the old comedy movie made Sarah and her friends laugh until they cried, reminiscing about their favorite scenes.
The unexpected antics of the clown at the children's birthday party had the kids laughing until they cried with delight.
During the office party, the hilarious office pranks shared by coworkers had them laughing until they cried, creating a memorable evening of merriment.

senses.definition: to laugh really loudly and in length
to laugh really hard, particularly to the point that one's stomach hurts

examples.x: The stand-up comedian's performance was so hilarious that the audience nearly busted a gut from laughing so hard.
When they watched the comedy show together, they all laughed so much that they thought they might bust a gut.
Jane's witty jokes had her friends laughing so intensely that they feared they would bust a gut.
During the family gathering, the funny stories and anecdotes had everyone laughing so hard that they thought they might bust a gut.
The unexpected comedic twist in the movie had the whole cinema laughing so heartily that it seemed like they might bust a gut.

senses.definition: (of laughter) in a very intense and uncontrollable manner

examples.x: The comedian's witty performance had the entire audience in stitches, with laughter filling the room.
When Sarah shared her funny travel stories, her friends were in stitches, unable to stop laughing.
The children watched the clown's hilarious antics and were in stitches, giggling uncontrollably.
During the sitcom's latest episode, the humorous plot twists had the viewers in stitches from start to finish.
John's impression of his teacher was so spot-on that his classmates were in stitches, unable to contain their laughter.
The comedy show was so hilarious that the entire audience erupted in belly laughs, filling the theater with mirth.
When the children watched the clown's performance, their belly laughs could be heard from across the circus tent.
The unexpected twist in the plot of the sitcom led to uproarious belly laughs from the viewers.
John's amusing anecdotes had his coworkers sharing belly laughs during their lunch break, brightening up the workday.
Charles was sitting in his place, grinning like a Cheshire cat, when I joined him.
When Sarah received the surprise gift, she couldn't help but grin like a Cheshire cat, her face beaming with delight.
As he revealed his well-kept secret, John's face lit up with excitement, and he grinned like a Cheshire cat.
The mischievous child grinned like a Cheshire cat after playing a prank on his siblings.
During the magical performance, the magician's assistant grinned like a Cheshire cat, adding to the mystique of the show.

word_list.pronunciation: ɡɹˈɪn smˈaɪl fɹʌm ˈɪɹ tʊ ˈɪɹ

senses.definition: (of a person) to look extremely happy and satisfied

examples.x: When Jane received the news of her promotion, she was grinning from ear to ear all day long.
As the couple exchanged their wedding vows, they smiled from ear to ear, radiating their happiness.
After winning the championship, the athlete was grinning from ear to ear during the awards ceremony.
Seeing her childhood friend after many years, Sarah was smiling from ear to ear, overwhelmed with joy and nostalgia.
The children couldn't hide their excitement on Christmas morning, as they smiled from ear to ear while opening their gifts.

senses.definition: a very charismatic smile that someone, particularly a woman, has

examples.x: The actress's million-dollar smile was one of her most distinctive features, contributing to her popularity on the big screen.
During the job interview, Tom flashed his million-dollar smile, leaving a positive impression on the hiring manager.
The supermodel's million-dollar smile graced the cover of numerous fashion magazines, making her a sought-after figure in the industry.
The salesperson used her million-dollar smile to win over potential clients and close important deals.
The politician's million-dollar smile helped him connect with voters and gain their trust during his campaign.
